title: Taragarh Fort
tagline: Taragarh Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Taragarh Fort is a fortress built upon a steep hillside in the city of Ajmer in the Indian state of Rajasthan.
overview.description: Taragarh Fort is a fortress built upon a steep hillside in the city of Ajmer in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It was constructed by Maharaja of Parmar Rajputs, and repaired by Ajayaraja Chauhan and it was originally called Ajaymeru Durg.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra
topPlaces.description: The Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra (literally "shed of 2½ days") is a historical former mosque in the city of Ajmer, in Rajasthan, India. It is one of the oldest mosques in India, and the oldest surviving monument in Ajmer.

topPlaces.name: Shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti
topPlaces.description: The Shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti, also known as the Ajmer Dargah Shareef, is a Sufi dargah complex incorporating the shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti, several tombs, and a mosque, located at Ajmer, in the state of Rajasthan, India. The shrine is significant and it is one of the most popular sites of religious visitation for Sunni Muslims in the Indian subcontinent that can attract up to 20,000 pilgrims per day, swelling to hundred of thousands on Chishti's urs.

topPlaces.name: Urs festival, Ajmer
topPlaces.description: The Urs festival is an annual festival held at Ajmer, Rajasthan, India which commemorates the anniversary of the death of Sufi saint Moinuddin Chishti (1143 - 1236) (founder of the Chishtiya Sufi order in India). This Sufi saint preached tolerance of all religions and gave a message of love.

topPlaces.name: Boraj-Kazipura, Ajmer
topPlaces.description: Boraj-Kazipura is a census town in Ajmer tehsil of Ajmer district of Rajasthan state in India. The census town and village falls under Hathi khera gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Bhawani Khera, Ajmer
topPlaces.description: Bhawani Khera is a village in Ajmer tehsil of Ajmer district of Rajasthan state in India. The village falls under Narwar gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Ajmer
topPlaces.description: Ajmer (pronounced [əd͡ʑ.meːɾ] ) is a city in the north-western Indian state of Rajasthan. It serves as the administrative headquarters of the Ajmer district and Ajmer division.

topPlaces.name: Ajmer State
topPlaces.description: Ajmer State was a separate state within India from 1950 to 1956 with Ajmer as its capital. Ajmer State was formed in 1950 out of the territory of former province of Ajmer-Merwara, which became a province of the Indian Union on 15 August 1947.

topPlaces.name: Ajmer Jain temple
topPlaces.description: The Ajmer Jain temple, also known as Soniji Ki Nasiyan, is a Jain temple known for its architecture. It was built in the late nineteenth century.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra & around

items.note: The Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra (literally "shed of 2½ days") is a historical former mosque in the city of Ajmer, in Rajasthan, India. It is one of the oldest mosques in India, and the oldest surviving monument in Ajmer.

The Shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti, also known as the Ajmer Dargah Shareef, is a Sufi dargah complex incorporating the shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti, several tombs, and a mosque, located at Ajmer, in the state of Rajasthan, India. The shrine is significant and it is one of the most popular sites of religious visitation for Sunni Muslims in the Indian subcontinent that can attract up to 20,000 pilgrims per day, swelling to hundred of thousands on Chishti's urs.

The Urs festival is an annual festival held at Ajmer, Rajasthan, India which commemorates the anniversary of the death of Sufi saint Moinuddin Chishti (1143 - 1236) (founder of the Chishtiya Sufi order in India). This Sufi saint preached tolerance of all religions and gave a message of love.
Boraj-Kazipura is a census town in Ajmer tehsil of Ajmer district of Rajasthan state in India. The census town and village falls under Hathi khera gram panchayat.
Bhawani Khera is a village in Ajmer tehsil of Ajmer district of Rajasthan state in India. The village falls under Narwar gram panchayat.
Ajmer (pronounced [əd͡ʑ.meːɾ] ) is a city in the north-western Indian state of Rajasthan. It serves as the administrative headquarters of the Ajmer district and Ajmer division.

Ajmer State was a separate state within India from 1950 to 1956 with Ajmer as its capital. Ajmer State was formed in 1950 out of the territory of former province of Ajmer-Merwara, which became a province of the Indian Union on 15 August 1947.
The Ajmer Jain temple, also known as Soniji Ki Nasiyan, is a Jain temple known for its architecture. It was built in the late nineteenth century.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Taragarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Taragarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Taragarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (126 km) and the nearest railway station is Ajmer Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Taragarh Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Taragarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Taragarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Taragarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Taragarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra, Shrine of Mu'in al-Din Chishti, Urs festival, Ajmer, Boraj-Kazipura, Ajmer, Bhawani Khera, Ajmer.
